requirement.original: 实施全部 (final user confirmation token at end of multi-turn debate with codex about systematically preventing /tmp or /dev/shm tmpfs from filling up again without changing existing architecture or functionality)
requirement.clarified: Implement the 4-layer additive plan documented in docs/dev/user-requirement-dev-20260519-161035.md: (L1) SessionStart df -h /tmp /dev/shm banner with >75% highlight; (L1.5) UserPromptSubmit non-blocking pressure warning with ≤3-per-session rate limit; (L2) extend /usr/local/sbin/tmp-cleanup.sh with EXCLUSION-FIRST ordering, full pattern coverage, and freed-bytes logging; (L3) document *-scratch-<ts> convention.
requirement.what: Four discrete work items implementing layered preventive infrastructure for tmpfs pressure
requirement.why: Recurrent /tmp (85%) and /dev/shm (69%) saturation events; existing daily cron recovered only 28K on 2026-05-19; need visibility, mid-session warning, broader cleanup coverage, and a future-proofing convention — without changing any subagent runtime behavior

root_cause_analysis.symptom: Recurrent /tmp and /dev/shm tmpfs saturation; existing daily cron only recovered 28K on 2026-05-19
root_cause_analysis.root_cause: N/A — this is an enhancement (preventive infrastructure), not a bug fix. The 'cause' of the tmpfs pressure is broadly the natural accumulation of Playwright profiles, codex outputs, hook state files, and dev/test scratch over time, against a 4G/16G tmpfs budget. Existing cleanup script's pattern list is too narrow (codex enumerated ~15 missing patterns) and lacks EXCLUSION-FIRST ordering (literal chrome-debug-profile in the prune list).
root_cause_analysis.why_problematic: Without broader pattern coverage and EXCLUSION-FIRST ordering, tmpfs continues to grow until manual intervention; risk of ENOSPC for subagents
root_cause_analysis.timeline: 2026-05-01 initial cron installed; 2026-05-19 user observed 85% /tmp utilization triggering the codex debate

pre_existing_guards.purpose: Strict bash mode — unset variable / pipe-failure detection
Dry-run mode gate — must be preserved for AC4/AC5 verifiability

pre_existing_guards.code_snippet: if [ "$DRY_RUN" -eq 1 ]; then "$@" -print 2>/dev/null | head -200; else "$@" -exec rm -rf {} + 2>/dev/null || true; fi
pre_existing_guards.purpose: Dry-run vs real-delete dispatch BEHAVIOR (per-category dispatch convention) is preserved. The exact CODE BODY of the run() helper MAY be rewritten — the current shape ("$@" -print and "$@" -exec rm -rf {} +) appends -print / -exec AFTER the supplied find expression, which is exactly the unsafe shape M3 forbids under EXCLUDE_PRUNE (the action would short-circuit the prune branch and act on excluded paths too). Dev MUST rewrite the helper so the -print / -print0 / -exec action lives INSIDE the right-hand non-prune branch (e.g., the helper builds `"$@" -o \( ... -print \)` shape directly, or each category writes its own full find invocation without using the helper). Codex F3 raised this contradiction explicitly.

Hook-state tier `-type f` predicate — preserved per AC7. This is the predicate that drives OBJ-3: M2's lock primitive MUST be `flock` (creates regular files) — atomic-`mkdir` (creates DIRECTORIES) is FORBIDDEN because lock directories would never match `-type f` and would accumulate indefinitely, defeating the rate-limit's leak-prevention guarantee. The M3 `claude-pressure-warn-*` pattern extension is added INSIDE this same hook-state tier and inherits the `-type f` predicate.

reference_source.description: User requirement document containing codex-debated, user-confirmed 4-layer additive plan with explicit pattern list, threshold values, hard-exclusion list, and 8 ACs. BA has independently verified all empirical claims against live filesystem.

novelty_check.rationale: First cycle for this requirement; no prior attempts exist

context.recent_commits: ba64db2 chore(orphan): unattributed session changes; c411ef1 chore(.gitignore): untrack docs/; a03025c chore(docs): record qa artifacts; e78ce03/ea02581 auto-bulk: end-of-cycle commits

development_approach.strategy: Four parallel-implementable work items per the 4 layers; each is additive. The only existing-code removals are: (i) the chrome-debug-profile prune-target literal at tmp-cleanup.sh:61, (ii) the happy-attachments prune-target literal at tmp-cleanup.sh:86, and (iii) the unconditional exec-redirect at tmp-cleanup.sh:28 (replaced with a DRY_RUN-conditional that preserves logging for real runs but emits dry-run candidates to stdout via the SOLE acceptable tee-to-BOTH-stdout-AND-log shape per OBJ-2). All three removals are required per QA objections in BA-QA iteration 1; the two path literals remain ACTIVELY PROTECTED via the shared EXCLUDE_PRUNE clause introduced by M3. Implementation order: L1 → L1.5 → L2 → L3 (independent; can also be reordered). Key correctness invariants per codex review and QA iteration-1+2 objections: (a) find -prune action MUST live inside right-hand branch (not appended after whole expression — would leak); (b) -path '*debug-profile*' MUST be in shared exclusion clause used by every TOP-LEVEL /tmp find (carve-out: the /tmp/claude-0 deep .output query is the sole intentional exception and MUST NOT use EXCLUDE_PRUNE — comment this in the script); (c) claude-0/**/*.output query MUST be -mindepth 2 -type f recursive (real depth is 6, not 2 — AC5 wording revised to drop the wrong 'DIRECTLY under /tmp/claude-0' phrasing and use the explicit find command); (d) du -sh --max-depth=1 is broken on GNU du; use du -xh --max-depth=1 with awk filter; (e) session token MUST come from stdin JSON session_id, not /tmp/claude-0 mtime; (f) counter file MUST use atomic update via `flock` ONLY (atomic-`mkdir` is FORBIDDEN per OBJ-3 because lock directories would never match the M3 `-type f` hook-state predicate and would leak indefinitely) and the rate-limit decision MUST be propagated from the lock-protected critical section to the parent shell via command substitution. **fd 9 MUST be opened INSIDE the command substitution via a redirected brace-group** (per iteration-2 codex F1): `decision=$( { flock -x 9 || { printf 'skip'; exit 0; }; n=$(cat counter || echo 0); if [ "$n" -ge 3 ]; then printf '%s\n' "$n" > counter; printf 'skip'; else echo $((n+1)) > counter; printf 'emit'; fi; } 9> lockfile ); [ "$decision" = "emit" ] || exit 0`. The trailing-redirection form `decision=$( ... ) 9> lockfile` is REJECTED — fd 9 may not be reliably visible to commands inside the command substitution in that form. Bare `exit 0` inside the subshell only exits the subshell, the parent hook would still emit the warning (OBJ-1). In the saturated-counter branch (n>=3), the counter file's mtime MUST be refreshed by rewriting the same value (codex F7) so the >7d hook-state sweep cannot delete an actively-saturated counter mid-session and reset the ≤3/session guarantee for long-lived sessions. Both counter file and paired `.lock` file are swept by L2 via the existing `claude-pressure-warn-*` wildcard at the >7d hook-state tier (both are regular files because `flock` creates regular files); (g) *-scratch-[0-9]* MUST be in L2 pattern list so L3 documentation is truthful; (h) SessionStart banner emits ONE LINE PER MOUNT (two lines total, derived from df -h | tail -n +2) — WARN prefix per-line, per-mount (resolves AC1-vs-M1 form contradiction surfaced by QA objection #2); (i) UserPromptSubmit warning emits ONE top-5 dirs block PER pressured mount (so two blocks when both mounts are over threshold — resolves AC3 both-mounts ambiguity surfaced by QA objection #5); (j) tmp-cleanup.sh --dry-run MUST tee candidate enumeration to BOTH stdout AND log (the SOLE acceptable shape per OBJ-2 — stdout-only / log-only / 'dev choice' all explicitly rejected); (k) AC6 freed_total formula MUST sum BOTH /tmp delta AND /var/tmp/codex-outputs delta (per OBJ-4: the user's intent is the total freed by this script's run, not /tmp-only — /var/tmp/codex-outputs >7d sweep IS one of the categories AC6 'all categories' covers); (l) M2 hook MUST resolve df/du via PATH lookup (bare `df` / `du` — NOT `/usr/bin/df` / `/usr/bin/du`) per OBJ-5 so the QA PATH-shim test driver can replace them for synthetic >75% scenarios without risking real tmpfs fill; QA driver MUST use DISTINCT session_id per iteration to avoid counter saturation masking shim variation.
files_to_create: docs/reference/tmp-cleanup-convention.md (Layer 3 — OR append section to docs/reference/server-infra.md; dev chooses)
files_to_modify: settings.json (repo) / /root/.claude/settings.json (runtime) (register both new hooks — append 6th entry to SessionStart hooks block; append new (4th) hook block to UserPromptSubmit hooks array)

/usr/local/sbin/tmp-cleanup.sh (Layer 2 — EXCLUSION-FIRST shared clause + carve-out comment; rewrite run() helper body so the -print / -print0 / -exec action lives inside the right-hand non-prune branch [codex F3: existing append shape is unsafe with EXCLUDE_PRUNE]; extended patterns across 3 age tiers; per-category 'what it removed' logging [codex F6: either candidate-paths-before-rm or per-category removed_count line]; IEC-i-formatted freed-bytes total log line summed across /tmp AND /var/tmp/codex-outputs deltas [per OBJ-4]; condition line-28 exec-redirect on DRY_RUN=0 via tee-to-stdout-AND-log [SOLE acceptable shape per OBJ-2]; remove chrome-debug-profile literal from line 61; remove happy-attachments literal from line 86)
development_approach.validation_approach: QA verifies AC1-AC2 by inspecting the new session-tmpfs-banner.sh hook's stdout in a fresh session — exactly two mount lines must appear from THAT hook (the existing 5 SessionStart hooks emit their normal output separately); the literal 'WARN ' prefix (5 bytes including trailing space) must be applied per-line to over-threshold mounts only. AC3 by PATH-shim mocking (per OBJ-5): (a) the M2 hook MUST resolve df/du via PATH lookup — bare `df`/`du` invocations, NOT `/usr/bin/df` / `/usr/bin/du` / `command df` / `command du` — verifiable by grep against the hook source (any absolute-path invocation of df/du in userprompt-tmpfs-pressure.sh is an AC3 fail). (b) Test driver prepends a temp dir to PATH containing executable shims that emulate df/du with synthetic high-usage output (e.g., a `df` shim that prints fake `Use%` of 85% for /tmp); a corresponding `du` shim emits canned top-5 dir lines. (c) Test driver invokes the userprompt-tmpfs-pressure.sh hook directly with stdin JSON; each iteration uses a DISTINCT session_id (e.g., `ba-qa-iter1`, `ba-qa-iter2`, `ba-qa-iter3`, `ba-qa-iter4`) so per-session counters reset — reusing one session_id across iterations would saturate the SAME counter from iteration 4 onward and mask shim-output variation across iterations. With distinct session_ids the expected behavior is: each iteration emits the warning exactly once (the rate-limit is per-session, four distinct sessions = four warnings). To verify the rate-limit itself, a separate FIFTH test uses the SAME session_id for 4 consecutive submissions and asserts emissions on iterations 1-3 only (4th is silent — counter saturated at 3). Verify both single-mount and both-mounts cases by varying the df-shim's output. Do NOT use live-fill validation (do NOT create large dummy files in /tmp or /dev/shm to force real >75% — that approach risks triggering the exact ENOSPC failure mode this work is supposed to prevent, per codex F9). AC4 by running '/usr/local/sbin/tmp-cleanup.sh --dry-run' and grepping STDOUT (primary channel — verifiable because line-28 exec-redirect is conditioned on DRY_RUN=0 via the tee-to-stdout-AND-log shape, the SOLE acceptable dry-run shape per OBJ-2); the same enumeration must also appear in /var/log/tmp-cleanup.log under the most-recent run's dry_run=1 block. AC5 by ensuring all hard-exclusion paths exist on disk and asserting none appear as deletion candidates AND that .output files matched by 'find /tmp/claude-0 -mindepth 2 -type f -name *.output -mtime +1' (recursive, depth ≥2) MAY appear (the documented carve-out). AC6 (per OBJ-4 + codex F6) by triggering one real run and inspecting /var/log/tmp-cleanup.log for BOTH (i) a `freed_total=` line whose value is the IEC-i-formatted sum of the /tmp AND /var/tmp/codex-outputs deltas (clamped to 0 for negatives) — a /tmp-only formula does NOT satisfy AC6, AND (ii) per-category 'what was removed' evidence (either candidate-paths-before-rm enumeration OR a per-category `removed_count=N` summary line) for every sweep category that fired in this run. AC7 by spot-checking that the existing banner content + UserPromptSubmit hook ordering + /tmp/chrome-debug-profile + /tmp/happy-attachments preservation AND the five existing tmp-cleanup sweep categories (codex artifacts >1d, Playwright profiles >1d, hook state >7d still using `-type f` predicate per OBJ-3 — which is why M2 mandates `flock` over atomic-`mkdir` — dev/test scratch >3d, /var/tmp/codex-outputs >7d) all hold. AC8 by grepping docs/reference/ for the convention note that explicitly states *-scratch-[0-9]* is swept at the >3d tier.

codex_consult.feedback_summary: Three codex consults: iteration-0 returned 8 findings (1 blocker, 6 major, 1 observation_only), all 7 actionable applied. Iteration-1 (during BA-QA iteration 1) returned 10 additional findings (F1-F10), all applied. Iteration-2 (this session, during BA-QA iteration 2/3 after the 5 QA objections were fixed) returned 7 more findings: 1 blocker (F1: fd 9 inside command substitution) + 6 major (F2: dual-path nomenclature; F3: run() helper rewrite framing; F4: optional exclusion-block clarification; F5: cron-user rule scope; F6: AC6 'what was removed' logging; F7: saturated-counter mtime refresh). All 7 iteration-2 findings classified in_scope_real_bug or in_scope_minor per Rule 3, all applied.

findings.issue: find -prune cannot be injected into the run() helper safely — action -print or -exec MUST live inside the right-hand non-prune branch, never appended after the whole expression
findings.proposed_fix: find /tmp -maxdepth 1 \( -path EXCL1 -o -path EXCL2 -o ... -o -path '*debug-profile*' \) -prune -o \( <age/type/name predicates> -print \); for real delete use -print0 | xargs -0r rm -rf -- OR put -exec rm -rf {} + inside the right-hand branch
findings.rationale: Without this fix the EXCLUSION-FIRST guarantee would be silently violated; spec Technical Hints and Edge Cases updated to make this structural shape explicit

findings.issue: *debug-profile* protection must be global across every /tmp sweep, not only the Playwright-profile group
findings.proposed_fix: Place -path '*debug-profile*' inside the shared hard-exclusion branch reused by every find /tmp ... cleanup category before all age/name predicates
findings.rationale: Avoids future renamed debug profiles being matched by *-deploy / *-app-check / dev-* / qa-* tiers; spec M3 + Edge Cases now require the shared clause

findings.issue: BA's safety evidence for /tmp/claude-0/**/*.output used -maxdepth 2 but real files live at depth 6 (/tmp/claude-0/<project>/<session>/tasks/*.output)
findings.proposed_fix: Use find /tmp/claude-0 -mindepth 2 -type f -name '*.output' -mtime +1 (add -type f so directories named *.output cannot match)
findings.rationale: BA re-verified at depth 6: 392 .output files, 26 older than 1d, 17 currently-open per lsof, intersection (open AND >1d) = 0 — threshold still safe; spec Evidence + Edge Cases + Technical Hints + AC4 all corrected

findings.issue: du -sh --max-depth=1 is broken on GNU du (-s conflicts with --max-depth=1, emits warning instead of data)
findings.proposed_fix: timeout 5s du -xh --max-depth=1 "$mount" 2>/dev/null | awk -v m="$mount" '$2 != m' | sort -hr | head -5 || true
findings.rationale: Without this fix the AC3 top-5 dirs output would be empty; spec Edge Cases + Technical Hints + AC3 updated to mandate the awk-filter form

findings.issue: Rate-limit token derivation must use UserPromptSubmit stdin JSON session_id, not stat -c %Y /tmp/claude-0
findings.proposed_fix: Read stdin JSON like prompt-workflow.py does; prefer session_id, then CLAUDE_SESSION_ID env, then hashed PPID/process-start fallback; sanitize/hash the token before forming filename
findings.rationale: /tmp/claude-0 mtime is global/mutable so it would share counters across sessions or reset within a session; spec Edge Cases + Technical Hints + AC3 now mandate stdin-JSON token derivation

findings.issue: Counter file updates need atomic serialization AND the counter files themselves leak /tmp space the feature is supposed to protect
findings.proposed_fix: Use flock or atomic mkdir lock for counter updates; add claude-pressure-warn-* to the existing >7d hook-state cleanup tier in tmp-cleanup.sh
findings.rationale: Closes both races (>3 warnings possible without lock) and the meta-leak (Layer 1.5 must not consume the resource it protects); spec M2 + M3 + Edge Cases + AC3 + AC4 all updated

findings.issue: *-scratch-<ts> convention is documented but not actually swept by Layer 2 — AC8 would make a false claim
findings.proposed_fix: Add -name '*-scratch-[0-9]*' to the >3d cleanup tier (bounded by [0-9]* to avoid matching unrelated names)
findings.rationale: Without this fix the L3 convention would document an aspirational behavior that doesn't exist; spec M3 + AC4 + AC8 + Edge Cases all updated to make the documentation truthful

findings.issue: Confirms BA's plan to add a 4th UserPromptSubmit block (vs nesting into existing block) is the correct settings shape — existing 3 matcher-less blocks stack, appending a 4th adds rather than replaces
findings.rationale: Validates the chosen approach; no spec change needed

findings.issue: iteration-2 codex F1: fd 9 is not reliably visible inside the command substitution when the redirect is written AFTER as `decision=$( ... ) 9> "$lock_file"`; flock -x 9 can fail with Bad file descriptor and leave the critical section unsynchronized
findings.proposed_fix: Open fd 9 INSIDE the command substitution via a redirected brace-group: `decision=$( { flock -x 9 || { printf 'skip'; exit 0; }; n=$(cat "$counter_file" 2>/dev/null || echo 0); if [ "$n" -ge 3 ]; then printf '%s\n' "$n" > "$counter_file"; printf 'skip'; else echo $((n+1)) > "$counter_file"; printf 'emit'; fi; } 9> "$lock_file" ); [ "$decision" = "emit" ] || exit 0`. The trailing-redirection form is REJECTED.
findings.rationale: Without this fix the iteration-2 OBJ-1 'fix' would still silently allow unsynchronized counter updates under load; M2 Tech Hint + AC3 + strategy all updated to require the brace-group shape and reject the trailing-redirection shape.

findings.issue: iteration-2 codex F2: context JSON repeatedly used `.claude/settings.json` and `.claude/hooks/...` paths that conflict with the actual repo layout — the workspace root IS `/dev/shm/dev-workspace/dot-claude` (symlinked from /root/.claude), so repo-tracked files live at `settings.json` and `hooks/...` (no `.claude/` prefix). The `.claude/` prefix would address a different/nested directory.
findings.proposed_fix: Canonicalize all path mentions to `<repo-relative> (repo) / <runtime-absolute> (runtime)` dual-path form: settings.json (repo) / /root/.claude/settings.json (runtime); hooks/session-tmpfs-banner.sh (repo) / $HOME/.claude/hooks/session-tmpfs-banner.sh (runtime); etc.
findings.rationale: Eliminates ambiguity about where dev should write the new hooks and edit the existing settings; all relevant fields in context.json (requirement.where, scope_boundary, affected_files, observations, component_chain, scope_expansion, evidence.measured.location, development_approach.files_to_create / files_to_modify) updated to the dual-path form; ticket Scope section updated similarly.

findings.issue: iteration-2 codex F3: pre_existing_guards declared the line-39 run() helper 'must be preserved' with removal_authorized=false, but the helper's current shape appends `-print` / `-exec rm -rf` AFTER the caller's find expression — this is EXACTLY the unsafe shape M3 forbids under EXCLUDE_PRUNE (the action short-circuits the prune branch and acts on excluded paths too). Internal contradiction between the guard and the EXCLUSION-FIRST mandate.
findings.proposed_fix: Re-classify the line-39 guard: the DRY_RUN-vs-real DISPATCH BEHAVIOR is preserved (the per-category dispatch convention), but the helper's CODE BODY may be rewritten so the action lives inside the right-hand non-prune branch. Set removal_authorized to 'behavior_preserved_body_rewritable' and add a M3 Tech Hint bullet describing the rewrite.
findings.rationale: Without this fix dev would either preserve the unsafe append shape (silently violating EXCLUSION-FIRST per-category) or remove the guard wholesale (failing the AC7 behavior-preservation check). The rewrite-with-behavior-preserved framing resolves the contradiction; pre_existing_guards entry + M3 Tech Hint both updated.

findings.issue: iteration-2 codex F4: context constraint 'tmp-cleanup.sh dry-run must show preserved exclusions explicitly' contradicted ticket S1's 'OPTIONAL' status for the separate exclusion block — would make QA fail an implementation that satisfies AC5 but omits the optional audit block.
findings.proposed_fix: Reword constraint to: 'dry-run MAY show hard-exclusion paths in a separate labeled `=== excluded (NOT candidates) ===` block — OPTIONAL; AC5 is evaluated only against the deletion-candidate sections, NOT the optional exclusion block.'
findings.rationale: Aligns context constraint with ticket S1 canonical position; QA cannot fail an implementation that omits the optional exclusion block.

findings.issue: iteration-2 codex F5: 'Cron user: use absolute paths only' edge-case rule contradicted M3 Tech Hint snippets that use bare `find`/`df`/`numfmt`/`xargs`. Left dev/QA unclear whether bare commands fail the spec.
findings.proposed_fix: Soften the rule to: 'NEW additions to the M3 cron script MUST NOT depend on shell aliases, shell functions, sourced-rc state, or user-specific environment that wouldn't exist under cron's minimal PATH. Bare invocations of standard system commands that already resolve under cron's PATH on this server are acceptable. OBJ-5's bare-`df`/`du` PATH-shim requirement applies ONLY to the M2 hook (different context, different threat model).'
findings.rationale: Reconciles M2 OBJ-5 (PATH-shim) with M3 (cron context) without introducing an absolute-paths burden on M3 dev. Both contexts are unambiguous now.

findings.issue: iteration-2 codex F6: AC6 only enforced freed-bytes total, not the user requirement document's mandate to 'log what it removed AND how much space was freed' — current real mode could -exec rm -rf without logging the actual removed paths or counts.
findings.proposed_fix: Add an AC6 clause: 'For real runs, each sweep category MUST log EITHER the candidate paths selected for deletion (one path per line, before the actual rm) OR a per-category removed-count summary line (e.g., `category=playwright-profiles removed_count=12`). The existing `--- <desc> ---` category header line alone does NOT satisfy ‘actions taken’.'
findings.rationale: Closes the gap between AC6 and the user requirement doc line 53 ('what it removed and how much space was freed'); AC6 + context.development_approach.files_to_modify + validation_approach all updated.

findings.issue: iteration-2 codex F7: M2 ≤3/session rate-limit could be reset for long-lived sessions because the saturated branch (n>=3) didn't refresh the counter's mtime. M3 sweeps `claude-pressure-warn-*` at >7d; an active 8+ day session with a saturated counter would have its counter swept and start emitting from 0 again — silently breaking the lifetime guarantee.
findings.proposed_fix: In the n>=3 branch of the flock-protected critical section, rewrite the counter value (`printf '%s\n' "$n" > "$counter_file"`) to refresh mtime without incrementing. Explicitly note this is NOT slot consumption; it only prevents active saturated counters from being garbage-collected.
findings.rationale: Closes the long-lived-session reset hole; M2 Tech Hint + AC3 + strategy all updated to require the mtime refresh in the saturated branch.

codex_consult.feedback_incorporated: All 7 actionable findings from the iteration-0 codex (1 blocker + 6 major + 1 observation_only) applied. Iteration-1 codex F1-F10 applied. Iteration-2 codex F1-F7 (1 blocker + 6 major, all in_scope_real_bug or in_scope_minor per Rule 3 classification) all applied — fd-9-inside-command-substitution shape (F1), dual-path nomenclature (F2), run() helper rewrite framing (F3), optional exclusion-block clarification (F4), cron-user rule scoped to M3-only (F5), AC6 'what was removed' logging requirement (F6), saturated-counter mtime refresh (F7).
